lesson.menu_desc: Progress Check: self-assessment with can / can\'t -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 8 -- Block 1 Review -- Progress Check
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Aline Sberci | Aula 8 -- Block 1 Review

listenings.text: Hello Aline, this is a short progress report after your first seven lessons. I am really happy with your effort. You can now introduce yourself, talk about your daily routine, and describe your career. Last week you gave clear directions around the city, and your pronunciation is getting better. Your biggest strength is listening, because you understand fast speech well. One weakness to work on is writing longer sentences, but that is normal at this stage. Remember, every small mistake is a step forward. Lesson eight is an important milestone, so be proud of your progress. Keep your study habit strong, read my feedback, and bring one question to our next class. Great work, and talk soon.

Hi, this is just a note to myself about my English progress. When I started, I could only say a few words. Now I can talk about my work, my plans, and my last trip. I can give directions and order coffee without help. I feel more confident every week. My strength is speaking, because I am not afraid of mistakes. My weakness is grammar, so I need more practice with that. My goal for the next block is to write a clear email and join a short meeting in English. I am proud of my progress so far. Reaching lesson eight is a real milestone, and it took a lot of effort. I will keep going, one habit at a time.

extra_audio.text: First, Aline opens her notebook and reads the teacher’s feedback. Then she lists her strengths and her weakness. After that, she practices the hard words out loud. Next, she records herself and checks her mistakes. Finally, she sets one new goal and feels proud of her progress.
